description: Binary assertions for automated quality evaluation of learn-from-video output

tests.name: short_tutorial_video
tests.prompt: Create learning notes from this video: [INSERT_SHORT_TUTORIAL_URL]
tests.note: Replace with an actual short tutorial video URL (under 10 minutes) for real testing

assertions.text: Output is a valid .docx file that passes validate.py
Document contains a cover page with video title, URL, channel/source, date, and duration
Document contains a Table of Contents (manual, not empty widget)
Document contains an Executive Summary section under 500 words
At least 5 screenshots are embedded in the document via ImageRun
Every embedded screenshot has a caption with timestamp
No sections in the document are empty (zero content paragraphs)
All code blocks have a language label (JavaScript, Python, bash, etc.)
Code blocks captured from video are marked with FROM VIDEO [timestamp]
Code blocks with added completions are marked with ADDED FOR COMPLETENESS
At least one Mermaid diagram is present if the video showed any architecture/flow
Document contains a Quick Reference section with tables
Document contains a Source Videos & Resources section with clickable URL
Header contains video title and 'learnFromVideo Notes'
Footer contains page numbers
Document uses Arial font as default body text
Headings use the correct color scheme (H1: 1B4F72, H2: 2E75B6)
Tables use WidthType.DXA (not PERCENTAGE) and ShadingType.CLEAR (not SOLID)
Bullet lists use LevelFormat.BULLET numbering (not unicode bullet characters)
Video file is deleted after frame extraction (not left on disk)

tests.name: code_heavy_video
tests.prompt: Create learning notes from a coding tutorial video
tests.note: Use a video with significant code shown on screen (programming tutorial, live coding, etc.)

assertions.text: At least 3 code blocks are captured from video screenshots
Code blocks include proper imports and are syntactically complete
Each major code block has an explanation of what it does
Code appears INLINE where it is discussed (not in a separate chapter)
Screenshots of code are embedded alongside the transcribed code block

tests.name: multi_video_topic
tests.prompt: Create combined learning notes from 2 videos on the same topic
tests.note: Use two videos covering overlapping content to test merge behavior

assertions.text: Document has a Video Sources table listing both videos
Content is organized by THEME not by video
Video references [Video 1] [Video 2] appear where content overlaps
A comparison table exists for content covered by both videos
Executive Summary covers key takeaways from BOTH videos combined

scoring.scoring_method: count true assertions out of total

self_improvement.instructions: See references/self_improve_prompt.md for the autonomous improvement loop template
self_improvement.layer_1: Skill activation — test trigger phrases to optimize YAML description field
self_improvement.layer_2: Output quality — use Karpathy auto research loop with this eval.json
